Library Rules

  • Maintain Silence: Please keep noise to a minimum to create a conducive environment for studying and reading.
  • No Bags/ Handbags/ Raincoats/ Jerkins/ Casual Wears/ Printed Materials will be allowed inside the Library.
  • Note Books, and Laptops (without bags) however, may be allowed.
  • Handle Books with Care: Treat library materials with respect. Avoid marking, folding, or damaging books.
  • Use Library Resources Responsibly: Return borrowed items on time and report any damages or losses to the library staff.
  • Electronic Devices: Use electronic devices on silent mode and avoid disturbing others. Follow library guidelines for device usage.
  • Respect Library Staff: Follow instructions from library staff and seek assistance when needed. They are there to help you.
  • Library Hours: Adhere to the library's operating hours and plan your visits accordingly.
  • Students should Keep Silence in the Library. Mobile Phones should be kept in "Silent Mode" while using Library.
  • Maximum number of Books that can be issued is 2.
  • Periodicals and Reference Books will not be issued and may be consulted only in the Library.
  • A Book must be returned in the same condition in which it was issued.
  • Personal Belongings: Keep personal belongings with you at all times. The library is not responsible for lost or stolen items.
  • Absence from the Institute will not be accepted as an excuse for the delay in returning of books.
  • In case of marking / underlining of books, the cost of the latest edition of the book will be charged from the student.
  • Any violation of the above Rules shall attract disciplinary action.
  • Food and Drinks: Refrain from bringing food and beverages into the library to prevent damage to materials and maintain cleanliness.
